name: Triple Berry Cobbler
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Blackberry Royal Wine (Mogen David brand)
5 cups Berries - fresh or unsweetened frozen
2 tsp Fresh orange peel - finely grated
1/4 cup Butter or margarine (cut into small bits)
Vanilla ice cream OR whipped cream (lightly sweetened)
recipeInstructions:
Choose any combination of 3: blackberries, Olallieberries, raspberries, boysenberries and blueberries.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a medium saucepan, stir together wine, sugar, and cornstarch until the cornstarch is completely dissolved.
Cook and stir over medium heat until the mixture bubbles and thickens.
Stir in berries and orange peel; stir until the berries are hot.
Pour the mixture into an 11-by-7-by-2-inch baking dish or 2-quart casserole.
To make topping, combine flour, sugar, and baking powder in a bowl. With a fork, cut butter into the flour mixture until it is crumbly. Stir in milk (the mixture will be lumpy).
Drop batter by spoonfuls onto the berries.
Bake for 20 to 30 minutes until the topping browns.
Serve cobbler warm with ice cream or whipped cream.
Makes 6 servings.
